examples.title: Selection Sort Algorithm
examples.description: In-place sorting algorithm that repeatedly selects minimum element
examples.code:
SELECTION-SORT(A, n)
BEGIN
 FOR i ← 0 TO n-2 DO
 minIndex ← i
 FOR j ← i+1 TO n-1 DO
 IF A[j] < A[minIndex] THEN
 minIndex ← j
 END IF
 END FOR
 SWAP A[i] AND A[minIndex]
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: Outer loop runs n-1 times. Inner loop finds index of minimum element in unsorted portion. After finding minimum, it swaps with the first unsorted position.

examples.title: Optimized Bubble Sort
examples.description: Bubble Sort with early termination using flag variable
examples.code:
BUBBLE-SORT(A, n)
BEGIN
 FOR i ← 0 TO n-1 DO
 swapped ← FALSE
 FOR j ← 0 TO n-i-2 DO
 IF A[j] > A[j+1] THEN
 SWAP A[j] AND A[j+1]
 swapped ← TRUE
 END IF
 END FOR
 IF NOT swapped THEN
 BREAK // Array is already sorted
 END IF
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: The swapped flag detects if any swaps occurred in a pass. If no swaps, array is sorted and algorithm terminates early, giving O(n) best case.

examples.title: Basic Bubble Sort (Unoptimized)
examples.description: Simple Bubble Sort without early termination
examples.code:
BUBBLE-SORT-BASIC(A, n)
BEGIN
 FOR i ← 0 TO n-2 DO
 FOR j ← 0 TO n-i-2 DO
 IF A[j] > A[j+1] THEN
 temp ← A[j]
 A[j] ← A[j+1]
 A[j+1] ← temp
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: Simple version without optimization. Always performs all passes regardless of whether array becomes sorted earlier. O(n²) in all cases.

examples.title: Trace Example - Selection Sort
examples.explanation: Shows step-by-step progression with 4 passes (n-1). After each pass, one more element is placed in its final sorted position from the beginning.
